description: Canonical record of thesis-validated imputation methods and forecasting models approved for production deployment in the HARIBON HAB detection system

imputation.justification: Best downstream HAB detection performance among all evaluated methods; achieves mean AUC of 0.794 across productive rolling-origin splits (1, 2, 4). Adaptive design selects imputation strategy (temporal interpolation for short gaps, spatial for long gaps, climatological for seasonal) based on detected gap pattern, preventing the statistical artifacts that plague purely spatial methods. Robust to operational variability in gap types and monitoring conditions.

fallback_method.name: Climatological Substitution
fallback_method.when_to_use: When test window contains very low bloom prevalence (low positive class representation); when positive-class predictions are required (AUC alone insufficient); when robustness across distributional shift is prioritized

forecasting.justification: Best ensemble strategy by AUC (0.7248) with significantly higher precision (0.6325) compared to stacked (0.4023) and individual models. More robust for production deployment due to resistance to distribution shift across rolling-origin splits. Stacked ensemble was evaluated but discarded due to poor generalization (AUC 0.6994) caused by meta-learner distribution shift across temporal splits.

ensemble_strategy.description: Weighted average of base model probability predictions using AUC-proportional weights: LSTM=0.7357 GRU=0.6990, Transformer=0.7000, XGBoost=0.7077. Weights are normalized to sum to 1.

alternative_strategies_evaluated.name: stacked
alternative_strategies_evaluated.rationale: Discarded — LogisticRegression meta-learner failed to generalize across rolling-origin splits due to distribution shift

alternative_strategies_evaluated.name: soft_vote
alternative_strategies_evaluated.rationale: Uniform averaging of probability predictions; baseline ensemble method

imputation_used_for_evaluation.note: All models (individual and ensemble) evaluated on data imputed using the primary imputation method above. This ensures consistency between thesis evidence and product operation.

pipeline_sequence.name: Data Load & Imputation
pipeline_sequence.output: Imputed feature matrix (16 variables, all gaps filled)

pipeline_sequence.name: Feature Engineering
pipeline_sequence.method: Lag features, rolling statistics, derived indices (as per Task 4)

pipeline_sequence.name: Ensemble Weighted Average Inference
pipeline_sequence.method: Load LSTM, GRU, Transformer, XGBoost → Generate probabilities → Weighted average by AUC scores
pipeline_sequence.output: Final HAB risk probability + confidence interval (based on ensemble std across splits)

pipeline_sequence.name: Decision Threshold Application
pipeline_sequence.method: Probability → Binary HAB class (optimize for recall in early warning context)

backend_integration_notes: Update product/haribon_system/backend/app/core/config.py to load this manifest at startup
Modify product/haribon_system/backend/app/scripts/daily_updater.py to read 'primary_method' and 'primary_model' from manifest instead of hardcoded values
Ensure all artifact paths are relative to repo root or environment-variable-configurable
Add manifest version check before inference to prevent stale model deployment

testing_performed: Consistent evaluation across all 11 imputation methods using identical XGBoost training pipeline
Blind ensemble comparison (no overfitting to final data)
Stability analysis across splits with different bloom prevalence and environmental conditions
known_limitations: Class imbalance in HAB labels; many splits have low positive-class representation (F1 = 0.0 for most individual methods)
Spatial Kriging and Temporal-Spatial Ensemble not evaluated downstream; potential for improvement not captured
Transformer model shows high variance across splits (AUC std = 0.197); consider ensemble uncertainty quantification
Evaluation period ends 2026-05-12; monitor performance drift if environmental conditions or monitoring gaps change
recommended_monitoring: Track actual bloom occurrence vs. predicted probabilities monthly; flag if AUC drifts below 0.70
Re-evaluate ensemble weights if new base models added
Re-run rolling-origin evaluation annually with new year's data as latest split
Monitor for data quality issues that might violate the 'gap < 14 days' assumption

manifest_governance.created_by: Haribon Thesis Analysis Pipeline (Tasks 2–5, Ensemble Model)
manifest_governance.contact_for_updates: Canabo, Junsay, Ramiro, Sionosa, Villarin (thesis authors)
